Strip_Summary: POV through rearview mirror showing person in backseat. Dark car interior, night road.
Bold white text on dark: 'Now you turn the radio up when they cry.'
Driver stickman beside taxi on city street. Knows every road, knows which streets flood.
Stickman in taxi knowing passengers. Good at silence and waiting. Only skill that matters.
Driving with regulars. Tuesday woman. Old man with cane. Warm daytime taxi interior.
Stickman enjoying conversation but counting thin tips. Not enough. Haven't been for a while.
Someone noticing driver. Not the driving. The silence. Taxi afternoon.
Man in backseat handing phone to driver. No destination given, just a phone.
Instructions: charge it, drive when buzzed, don't ask where. Close-up of face.
Envelope examination in car. More than a month's pay. Before the first drive.
Two phones on belt. Neither has real number. Close-up of belt detail.
Driver leaning over sleeping daughter. Dim bedroom. Soap and detergent scent.
Whispering goodbye. Morning promise. Something a father says. Bedside warmth.
Walking down hallway. Flickering light. Should fix that. Won't.
Phone buzzing eleven PM. Coordinates. Pickup time. And a number.
Six. Don't know what it means yet. Phone screen glow.
Renting sedan downtown. Never same car. Never own car. Night lot.
Adjusting seat. Adjusting mirrors. Driving to coordinates. Night departure.
Person getting in at bus station. No bag, no name. Back seat silence.
Seat shifting. Thin clothes for the cold. Night outside window.
POV rearview showing passenger face. Almost saying something.
Word in throat. Their question not yours. Swallowing the impulse.
Driver gripping wheel. First time choosing not to know. Something small and wrong.
Dark highway. Headlights cutting narrow tunnel. Wrong maybe, unfamiliar.
Person breathing in backseat. Car keeps moving. And so do you.
Same coordinates, different car. Second run. Night.
Passenger asking where they're going. Driver silent. They wait.
Asking again quieter. Trying more. Radio turned up. Not loud. Just enough.
Bold text: 'Everyone does.'
Another run. Fourth or fifth. Stopped counting. Blur of trips.
Rearview: young person with closed fists. Younger than daughter. Hands pressed to thighs.
Tilting mirror to show road only. Five under limit. Road behind visible.
Gripping wheel ten and two. Don't look again. Night driving.
Checkpoint. Blue lights. Hands tighten. Slowing. Pulling over.
Officer leaning in. Flashlight on backseat. Smile. Practiced lie. Smooth delivery.
Officer checks backseat. Passenger silent. Waved through. Hands unclenching two miles later.
Phone buzzes: faster. Accelerator. Five over, then ten. Highway racing.
Number six. Cold ischemia. Six hours. The window.
Don't know science. Know what happens if late. Clock is real.
Bold text on black: 'You are racing decay.'
Broker calling same night. Yes before sentence ends. No thinking. Math simple.
Two runs. One night. Three thousand dollars.
More than a month of taxi rides and conversations and tips from the old man.
Sedan at gate. Figure in scrubs. Passenger walking in. No look back.
Gate closes. Driver waiting alone in car.
Trying to think about inside. Stopping. Gate closed.
Someone bringing cooler. Heavier than expected. Towel on seat. Cooler on towel.
Not looking at it. Not opening it. Dark backseat.
AC set eighteen degrees. Phone told once. Never changed.
Driving alone with cooler. No breathing. No shifting. Just weight.
Familiar highway. Curve where asphalt dips. Pothole where cooler slides.
Steering around pothole. Receipt on dashboard. Don't remember leaving it.
Parking lot behind warehouse. Cooler taken. Envelope handed. No counting, no looking.
Sitting in empty car. Backseat smells different. Plastic and cold.
Gas station. Approved one. No cameras. Scrolling phone.
Face recognized. Same angle as rearview. Missing persons post.
Someone looking for them. Phone number. Thumb hovering. Three seconds.
Scrolling past. Buying water bottle. That's it.
Close-up cooler shifting in backseat. Sound: dull, wet, heavy.
Reaching back adjusting towel. Other hand on wheel. Jaw tight.
Not sure when jaw tightened. Body betrayal. Micro-crack.
Bold text on black: 'No flinch. No pause. No thought.'
Highway stretching. Twenty minutes blank. Taillights disappearing.
POV rearview: cooler where person used to be. Checking cargo not face.
AC hum. Eighteen degrees. Only sound.
Home at four AM. Daughter asleep. Standing outside door.
Light through crack. She left it on. Always does when he's gone.
Hand on door handle. Not pushing. Hearing breathing.
Standing there. Ten seconds. Maybe longer.
Stepping back. Hands smell of wheel. Checking phone.
Two stickmen in sedan. Teaching trainee route. Map on dash.
Gas stations with no cameras. Speed: five under, never exact.
Towel. AC. Eighteen degrees. Systematic training.
Telling trainee: don't look in mirror too long. Hearing himself. Same as recruiter.
Checking rearview. Hoping for cooler. Coolers don't talk.
Don't cry. Don't say thank you. People are harder.
Eating alone. No calls. Used to talk to passengers. Liked conversation.
Trying to remember last conversation not in backseat of paid car. Can't.
Phone buzzing. Coordinates. Engine start. No time check.
Bold text: 'It's Tuesday. It's always Tuesday.'
Stickman at rearview mirror. Empty reflection. Used to see a face.
Bold text on dark: 'There is nothing back there you need to see.'
